skill.name: geopandas
skill.description: GeoPandas guidance for reading, transforming, analyzing, and visualizing geospatial vector data in Python.
skill.summary: Work with shapefiles, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, PostGIS, CRS transforms, spatial analysis, and map outputs using GeoPandas.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown examples, GeoPandas API names, and ordinary geospatial text. No executable scripts, prompt injection, malware intent, credential exfiltration, or hidden control flow were found. The only retained caution is documented remote data loading from HTTP and cloud paths, which is legitimate for GIS workflows.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are Markdown fenced code or inline package commands used as documentation examples. They are not executable skill scripts or hidden runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative detections across all flagged files. They are inside Markdown code fences or inline documentation, and no runnable command wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts point to geospatial documentation text and headings, not hash functions or encryption choices. No cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain GeoPandas descriptions, EPSG headings, or plotting text. They do not contain MD5, SHA1, cipher configuration, or related calls.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Geometry Introspection
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts correspond to geometry properties such as centroid, bounds, and validity checks. They inspect GeoDataFrame geometry, not the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines access geospatial geometry metadata and validation flags. There is no OS probing, environment discovery, or network host enumeration.

low_findings.title: Remote Data Loading Examples Require Source Trust
low_findings.description: The skill documents reading spatial data from HTTPS, S3, and Azure paths. This is legitimate for geospatial workflows, but users should avoid untrusted or sensitive remote sources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network examples are explicit and reviewed in context. They load user-selected geospatial data and do not include exfiltration endpoints or automatic execution.

content.value_statement: Geospatial vector analysis often fails when formats, projections, and geometry operations are mixed incorrectly. This skill gives focused GeoPandas guidance for loading data, managing CRS, analyzing spatial relationships, and creating maps.
actual_capabilities: Explains GeoSeries and GeoDataFrame structures for spatial vector data.
Shows how to read and write Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, Parquet, Feather, and PostGIS data.
Guides coordinate reference system checks, assignment, reprojection, and projection selection.
Covers buffers, centroids, hulls, simplification, affine transforms, and geometry properties.
Supports spatial joins, nearest joins, overlays, dissolve, clipping, indexing, distance, and area calculations.
Provides static and interactive mapping patterns with matplotlib, folium, contextily, and CartoPy.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install packages or run analysis by itself.
Examples use placeholder file paths, database URLs, and column names that users must replace.
Accuracy depends on choosing the correct CRS for each dataset and measurement task.
It focuses on vector data and does not cover raster processing workflows in depth.

use_cases.title: Prepare Local GIS Data
use_cases.description: Load shapefiles, GeoJSON, and GeoPackage layers, inspect CRS values, and export clean analysis-ready datasets.

use_cases.title: Build Spatial Analysis Pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine boundaries, points, and attributes with spatial joins, overlays, buffers, dissolve, and distance calculations.

use_cases.title: Create Maps for Reports
use_cases.description: Generate choropleth maps, layered static plots, interactive Folium maps, and publication-ready figure exports.

prompt_templates.title: Load My Spatial File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the geopandas skill to show how to load my GeoJSON file, inspect columns, check CRS, and save a GeoPackage copy.

prompt_templates.title: Fix CRS Before Measuring
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the geopandas skill to help me choose a projected CRS before calculating area and distance for my dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Join Points to Polygons
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the geopandas skill to design a workflow that joins point observations to administrative polygons and aggregates values by region.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Multi-Source GIS Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the geopandas skill to review my pipeline for reading PostGIS, GeoPackage, and remote layers, then improve CRS handling and performance.

output_examples.input: I need to calculate parcel areas from a shapefile.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends checking the current CRS, reprojecting to an appropriate projected CRS, calculating area, and exporting results to GeoPackage.

output_examples.input: I have store points and census polygons.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines a spatial join, validates matching CRS values, aggregates store counts by polygon, and suggests a choropleth map.

output_examples.input: I need an interactive map from a GeoDataFrame.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes using GeoPandas explore, selecting useful tooltip fields, setting a color column, and saving the map as HTML.

best_practices: Check CRS before every spatial join, overlay, distance, or area calculation.
Use projected CRS values for measurements and geographic CRS values for latitude and longitude display.
Filter rows, columns, and bounding boxes during reads when working with large spatial datasets.
anti_patterns: Do not calculate area or distance directly in EPSG:4326 coordinates.
Do not use set_crs to transform coordinates; use to_crs for reprojection.
Do not load remote spatial files unless the source is trusted and expected.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users apply GeoPandas for vector data loading, CRS handling, spatial analysis, geometry operations, and mapping.

faq.question: Does the skill run GeoPandas code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users or agents decide whether to run code in their own environment.

faq.question: Which spatial formats are covered?
faq.answer: It covers Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, Parquet, Feather, PostGIS, and file-like geospatial inputs.

faq.question: Can it help with coordinate reference systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains checking, setting, reprojecting, estimating UTM CRS, and choosing projections for measurements.

faq.question: Does it cover visualization?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes static plots, choropleth maps, layered maps, interactive maps, basemaps, and figure export patterns.

faq.question: What should users verify before using examples?
faq.answer: Users should verify file paths, trusted data sources, database credentials, column names, geometry validity, and CRS assumptions.
